stories.headline: Live updates: White House correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect wrote statement denouncing Trump
stories.summary: The shooting prompted the evacuation of President Donald Trump and other high-profile guests from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner Saturday. The suspect is in custody.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flamesThe US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-P

stories.headline: Asia's great disconnect: Markets boom as Iran war fuels cost of living crisis
Markets Pricing In 'Sooner Rather Than Later' End to Iran War: Amova
stories.summary: Amova Asset Management's Naomi Fink tells Bloomberg Television that the "markets are pricing in a sooner rather than later end to the hostilities" in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Correspondents’ dinner lacked highest security level despite presence of top officials
stories.summary: The White House correspondents’ dinner, which was attended by the president and many Cabinet members, was not given top security status that would have unlocked the full weight of federal resources.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Araghchi to meet Putin; Trump says Tehran can call for talks
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister heads to Russia as Trump says Iranian leaders can call on the phone if they want to talk.

stories.headline: Iran war tests role of Asian middle powers, says Thai ex-PM
At least 16 killed across Ukraine and Russia in exhange of strikes on Chernobyl anniversary
stories.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes on the city of Dnipro killed at least nine, Ukrainian authorities said Sunday, while Russian officials claimed one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone strike on the port city of Sevastopol.

stories.headline: Malik: UAE-US FX Swap a Backstop for Worst Case Scenario
stories.summary: The UAE's enquiry about a currency swap with the US is more about taking out an insurance policy rather than a sign of a fiscal crisis. That’s according to Hasnain Malik, Managing Director for Emerging Market Equity & Geopolitical Strategy at Tellimer who spoke to Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the UAE’s potential lifeline in case the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for longer. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Japanese Public Wants Energy-Saving Steps as Takaichi Holds Back
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East is putting pressure on the Japanese government to consider energy-saving measures, a challenge for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi as she seeks to calm public anxiety over potential shortages.

stories.headline: Japan eyes expanded warship exports with Mogami-class frigate as template
Rachel Reeves set for new growth push after May elections
stories.summary: Allies of UK chancellor seek to brush off talk of upheaval amid Labour Party unrest

stories.headline: Inside China’s plans to fight in space
stories.summary: From seizing satellites to striking Earth from orbit — Beijing is developing dual-use capabilities in an intensifying arms race with the US

stories.headline: Why the UAE asked Pakistan for its $3.5bn back
stories.summary: Tensions between Abu Dhabi and Islamabad have burst into the open

stories.headline: Sun Pharma Buys New Jersey-Based Organon in $12 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. has agreed to acquire New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon & Co., expanding its global reach with one of the biggest outbound deals by an Indian company.

stories.headline: India Revamps Trade Reporting System, Including Offshore Rupee
stories.summary: India’s central bank-supervised clearinghouse is making changes to its trade reporting system to bring it more in line with global standards and to make it easier for lenders to disclose their offshore rupee trades, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: China Taps Another Tool to Drain Excess Liquidity From Market
stories.summary: China’s central bank is taking a further step to rein in a liquidity glut in its financial system by reducing the amount of medium-term funds it lends out to banks.

stories.headline: Markets on Edge With Rates, Earnings, Iran in Focus | The Asia Trade 4/27/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China’s Metals Industry Racked Up Huge Profits in First Quarter
stories.summary: China’s metals industry began this year with its biggest profits in at least a decade as soaring prices for aluminum and copper handed the sector a major boost.

stories.headline: China Emerges From Oil Shock With Industry Profits Masking Split
stories.summary: China’s industrial firms ended the first quarter with faster growth in earnings, an acceleration that hid a widening split between companies under pressure from higher costs and others benefiting from rising oil prices and the global artificial intelligence boom.

stories.headline: S.Korea Trade Minister on Trade, Supply Chain (Correct)
stories.summary:
Yeo Han-koo, South Korean Trade Minister, says South Korea has moved into the commercial viability review stage for its trade agreement with the US. He says the country is also looking for new partners for trade, in a bid to diversify supply chains amid ongoing disruptions from the Middle East. He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade."

(This version corrects the spelling of the Kospi index) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: How Chinese EV Makers Are Winning Over Consumers
stories.summary: Legacy Western automakers once held the keys to China’s car market, but the 2026 Beijing Auto Show shows how domestic EV makers are taking the lead. Stephen Engle reports on how the latest tech and aggressive pricing are winning over consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Why DeepSeek V4 Impresses Despite Lack of 'Wow' Factor
stories.summary: China's DeepSeek has rolled out preview versions of a new flagship artificial intelligence model, calling it its most powerful open-source platform. Bloomberg Intelligence's Robert Lea explains how the Chinese flagship system is hampered in some areas by a lack of access to Nvidia tech but playing to its strengths in others. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Andy Haldane: The UK Economy Is Fixable (Here’s How)
stories.summary: Former Bank of England Chief Economist Andy Haldane joins Merryn Somerset Webb to unpack why the UK may feel poorer, more fragile and seemingly out of options — despite sitting on untapped wealth and world-class innovation. From tough fiscal trade-offs and rising taxes to the surprising strength of private balance sheets, he argues the UK’s problems may be serious — but they’re far from unsolvable. The big question: can policymakers u

stories.headline: A Silicon Rally to Knock Your SOX Off
stories.summary: The rise of semiconductor stocks over the last month has been breathtaking.

stories.headline: Emerging Stocks Rise to Record High on AI, Iran’s Hormuz Offer
stories.summary: Emerging-market equities rose to a record high, buoyed by optimism over the artificial intelligence trade and a report that Iran offered a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Tokyo condo boom cools off as interest rates rise
City of Beijing to bar consumer drone sales, tightening security in capital
BYD earnings, Japanese airline financials, Genting's New York casino tables
Islamabad residents bear cost of Pakistan's US-Iran mediation
Bank branches in Japan tapped to double as luggage hubs
Uzbekistan pushes digital, aiming to formalize shadow economy
Nvidia not competing with clients on AI models: vice president
Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Security footage posted by President Donald Trump on social media appears to show the shooting suspect running toward White House Correspondents' dinner. Moments later, officials evacuated the area as gunshots were reported outside the ballroom.

stories.headline: Abbas loyalists sweep Palestinian elections, including some seats in Gaza
stories.summary: Voter turnout in Gaza remained low due to the ongoing impact of the genocide.

stories.headline: After Shooting, Trump Demands Approval for His White House Ballroom
stories.summary: An incident involving an armed man at the White House correspondents’ dinner has the president to renewing his push for a project slowed by litigation.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner Trump Was Attending
stories.summary: A gunman carrying multiple weapons attempted to break into the dinner that President Trump was attending. Police have taken a suspect into custody.

stories.headline: Trump Praises WHCA President Weijia Jiang After Shooting at Dinner
stories.summary: Weijia Jiang, a CBS correspondent, had been sitting next to the president at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out. Soon afterward, she was reporting on the attack.

stories.headline: Correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect called himself ‘friendly federal assassin’
stories.summary: The suspect has been identified as Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of California, officials said. He is a part-time teacher, former engineering student and game designer.

stories.headline: Who is Cole Allen? What we know about White House press dinner shooting suspect
stories.summary: The man arrested in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner, attended by US President Donald Trump, has been identified by US media as a mechanical engineer from California.

stories.headline: Moment Trump rushed to safety as gunshots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump was quickly escorted to safety from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard at the venue.

stories.headline: Trump evacuated after shots fired near White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Following the incident, US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the alleged suspect who reportedly opened fire near the ballroom where the annual correspondents' dinner was being held.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
stories.summary: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Oil Rises as Hormuz Remains Shut After US-Iran Peace Talks Stall
stories.summary: Oil rose after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining virtually impassable, extending disruptions in the Middle East that have roiled global markets.

stories.headline: Security at Correspondents’ Dinner Worked as Intended, Experts Say
stories.summary: Officials noted that the suspect never made it into the hotel ballroom, where President Trump and hundreds of journalists were gathered for the White House correspondents’ dinner.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
stories.summary: Car bomb kills Sadio Camara at home during coordinated assaults by rebel groups including West African al-Qaida affiliateMali’s defence minister was killed in an attack on his residence, the government said on Sunday, a high-profile fatality during coordinated assaults staged the previous day by insurgents including the West African affiliate of al-Qaida.A car laden with explosives driven by a suicide attacker drove into Sadio Camara’s residence

stories.headline: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine
stories.summary: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine

stories.headline: Bomb attack on Colombia highway kills 19 ahead of election
stories.summary: Highway bombing in Colombia’s Cauca kills 19, injures dozens amid a surge in violence ahead of next month’s election.

stories.headline: Stocks Rise on Iran Offer Report, Oil Pares Gain: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks advanced and oil pared gains following a report that Iran offered the US a proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing concern that efforts to restart peace talks had stalled.

stories.headline: Renewable energy will boost national security and protect UK from sabotage, minister says
stories.summary: Widely dispersed wind farms and solar panels are harder to target than fossil fuel power stations, Michael Shanks saysRenewable energy will boost the UK’s national security and make the country more resilient against potential aggression or sabotage, the government’s energy minister has said.Michael Shanks said widely dispersed wind farms and solar panels were much harder to target than large-scale fossil fuel power stations. They are also not vu

stories.headline: Orbán associates rush to move wealth out of Hungary after election defeat
stories.summary: Incoming PM Péter Magyar accuses Fidesz-linked figures of trying to shield their wealth from accountabilityAlong the banks of the Danube, news that the Viktor Orbán era had come to an end set off an hours-long party. The joy echoed across Hungary as people traded hugs and high-fives. For some, however, the landslide loss set off a frantic scramble.Private jets allegedly laden with the spoils of those whose wealth swelled during Orbán’s 16 years i

stories.headline: Ryanair to shut Berlin base as it blames rise in German aviation tax
stories.summary: Trade union criticises airline’s plan to halve passenger numbers to the city as  ‘purely profit-oriented’  Ryanair is to shut its Berlin operating base and cut its winter schedule to the German capital in half, blaming soaring aviation taxes in the country.The Irish budget carrier said its relocation of seven aircraft to other centres would reduce its Berlin passenger numbers from 4.5 million to 2.2 million a year, with flights in and out of the

stories.headline: Mali rattled by ongoing armed attacks: What to know
stories.summary: Mali's security situation has deteriorated in recent years, as armed groups have carried out coordinated attacks.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
stories.summary: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since SeptemberThe US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations.The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

stories.headline: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
stories.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

stories.headline: 'Animals are traumatised too': Pet rescuers under fire in Ukraine
stories.summary: Animal rescue teams reveal the risks they take to rescue dogs, cats and other animals from the war in eastern Ukraine.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
stories.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfiresAs wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods.As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition: anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s

stories.headline: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
stories.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

stories.headline: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
stories.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

stories.headline: Philippines' Duterte to stand trial as ICC confirms crimes against humanity charges
stories.summary: Ex-president Rodrigo Duterte is accused of the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs".

stories.headline: US to allow firing squads, gas and electrocution for federal executions
stories.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

stories.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
stories.summary: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.headline: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.headline: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
stories.summary: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas marketsAt the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel.China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage. Now they

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
stories.summary: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking pointsFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastLabor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry.It’s understood the government has elected

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.
